            "language": "english",
            "review": "[h1] [b] A Classic of the Multiplayer FPS Genre [/b] [/h1]\n[b] Counter-Strike [/b] is a nostalgic look into the humble beginnings of what would over the years grow to be THE eSports tactical shooter franchise. Originally being a mod for 1998's Half-Life and then later having Valve acquire both the rights for it and its developers, it would become the very first game released on Steam during its debut and is still to this day played by thousands daily.\n\nHistory lesson aside, even after close to 23 years since the release of version 1.6 it is still a fun game. While there are no official servers, there are thousands of community-ran servers available to choose from (most of them Russian, of course) which can range from classic matches to heavily modded, completely unique gamemodes, but I don't recommend joining the first server you see willy-nilly. Look up a list of servers for the game and choose one from a trusted source that you want to play on, or just play with friends, or bots.\n\nWhile the graphics sure do look like they're from back in 2000 this does not affect how enjoyable the game will be for you, at least in my experience, as the foundation of a team-based, tactical shooter is solid, and this being ran in the same engine as Half-Life the movement is particularly smooth and very easy to run on lower specs.\n\nWhy buy this when you can just play [b]Counter-Strike 2[/b] instead? I dunno man, I'd say it's fun to see how Counter-Strike started and how it reached the point it is at today.",

            "review": "Used to do tournaments in these old stomping grounds. \r\nAmazing times were had here, history was made, we all died, and we all had fun. \r\nThis was the game that introduced me to gaming as a whole, I was 3 and in Bulgaria, on a Windows Vista Tower.
